source.name: _additional_properties
source.label: Additional properties
source.description: The JDBC properties used for interaction with data source (multi-line key=value format).

source.name: _auto_commit_mode
source.label: Auto-commit mode
source.description: Configure the stage to run in auto-commit mode. In auto-commit mode, the transaction is committed automatically after each statement is executed. When the connector writes records to the data source, the transaction is committed after each row is written to the data source. When the stage is configured to run multiple statements on each row, the transaction is committed after each statement is executed on the row

source.name: _before_after
source.label: Run before and after SQL statements
source.description: Select Yes to run SQL statements before and after data is accessed in the database.

source.name: _before_after._after_sql
source.label: After SQL statement
source.description: Enter the SQL statement to run once after all of the data is processed.

source.name: _before_after._after_sql._fail_on_error
source.label: Stop the job when after SQL statement fails
source.description: Select Yes to stop the job if the After SQL statement fails.

source.name: _before_after._after_sql._read_from_file_after_sql
source.label: Read After SQL statement from the file
source.description: Select Yes to read the SQL statement from the file that is specified in the After SQL statement property.

source.name: _before_after._after_sql_node
source.label: After SQL (node) statement
source.description: Enter the SQL statement to run once on each node after all of the data is processed on that node.

source.name: _before_after._after_sql_node._fail_on_error
source.label: Stop the job when after SQL (node) statement fails
source.description: Select Yes to stop the job if the After SQL (node) statement fails.

source.name: _before_after._after_sql_node._read_from_file_after_sql_node
source.label: Read After SQL (node) statement from file
source.description: Select Yes to read the SQL statement from the file that is specified in the After SQL (node) statement property.

source.name: _before_after._before_sql
source.label: Before SQL statement
source.description: Enter the SQL statement to run once before any data is processed.

source.name: _before_after._before_sql._fail_on_error
source.label: Stop the job when before SQL statement fails
source.description: Select Yes to stop the job if the Before SQL statement fails.

source.name: _before_after._before_sql._read_from_file_before_sql
source.label: Read Before SQL statement from file
source.description: Select Yes to read the SQL statement from the file that is specified in the Before SQL statement property.

source.name: _before_after._before_sql_node
source.label: Before SQL (node) statement
source.description: Enter the SQL statement to run once on each node before any data is processed on that node.

source.name: _before_after._before_sql_node._fail_on_error
source.label: Stop the job when before SQL (node) statement fails
source.description: Select Yes to stop the job if the Before SQL (node) statement fails.

source.name: _before_after._before_sql_node._read_from_file_before_sql_node
source.label: Read Before SQL (node) statement from file
source.description: Select Yes to read the SQL statement from the file that is specified in the Before SQL (node) statement property.

source.name: _begin_end_sql
source.label: Run begin and end SQL statements
source.description: Select Yes to run SQL statements every time when a transaction begins and every time before a transaction ends

source.name: _begin_sql
source.label: Begin SQL statement
source.description: Enter the SQL statement to run one time before any records are processed in the transaction

source.name: _enable_partitioned_reads
source.label: Enable partitioned reads
source.description: Select Yes to run the statement on each processing node. The [[node-number]], [[node-number-base-one]] and [[node-count]] placeholders in the statement are replaced on each processing node with the actual zero-based node index, one-based node index and total number of nodes, respectively

source.name: _enable_quoted_ids
source.label: Enable quoted identifiers
source.description: Select Yes to enclose the specified table name and column names on the links in quoting strings when SQL statements are generated. The connector queries the driver to determine the quoting string. If it fails to obtain this information from the driver, the connector uses the backtick (`) character as the quoting string. The default is No.

source.name: _end_of_wave
source.label: Mark end of wave
source.description: Select Yes to generate an end-of-wave record after each wave of records, where the number of records in each wave is specified in the Record count property. When the Record count property is set to 0, the end-of-wave records are not generated

source.name: _end_sql
source.label: End SQL statement
source.description: Enter the SQL statement to run one time in the transaction after all the records were processed in the transaction and before the transaction completes successfully

source.name: _generate_sql
source.label: Generate SQL at run time
source.description: Select Yes to automatically generate the SQL statements at run time.

source.name: _isolation_level
source.label: Isolation level
source.description: Specify how the connector manages statements in transactions. As soon as the connector establishes a connection and issues the first transactional statement, the connector implicitly starts a transaction that uses the specified isolation level

source.name: _java._heap_size
source.label: Heap size
source.description: Specify the maximum Java Virtual Machine heap size in megabytes.

source.name: _limit_rows._limit
source.label: Row limit
source.description: Enter the maximum number of rows that will be returned by the connector

source.name: _read_mode
source.label: Read mode
source.description: Select the mode that you want to use to read from the data source

source.name: _record_count
source.label: Record count
source.description: Specify the number of rows that the stage reads from or writes to the data source in a single transaction. When this property is set to 0, the transaction is committed only once on each processing node of the stage after the stage processes all the rows on that node. When rows arrive on the input link of the stage in waves, the Record count value applies to each wave separately

source.name: _run_end_sql_if_no_records_processed
source.label: Run end SQL statement if no records processed
source.description: Select Yes to run the End SQL statement irrespective of the number of records processed in the transaction. Select No to run the End SQL statement only if one or more records were processed in the transaction

source.name: _select_statement
source.label: Select statement
source.description: Enter a SELECT statement. The statement is used to read rows from the database.

source.name: _select_statement._other_clause
source.label: Other clause
source.description: Other clause

source.name: _select_statement._read_from_file_select
source.label: Read select statement from file
source.description: Select Yes to read the SELECT statement from the file that is specified in the SELECT statement property.

source.name: _select_statement._where_clause
source.label: Where clause
source.description: Where clause

source.name: _session._array_size
source.label: Array size
source.description: Array size

source.name: _session._character_set_for_non_unicode_columns
source.label: Character set for non-unicode columns
source.description: Select the character set option for the values of Char, VarChar and LongVarChar link columns for which the Extended attribute is not set to Unicode. If you select the Default option, the character set encoding of the engine host system locale is used. If you select the Custom option, you must provide the character set name to be used.

source.name: _session._character_set_for_non_unicode_columns._character_set_name
source.label: Character set name
source.description: Specify the name of the character set encoding for the values of Char, VarChar and LongVarChar link columns for which the Extended attribute is not set to Unicode.

source.name: _session._default_length_for_columns
source.label: Default length for columns
source.description: Enter the default length for the Char, NChar, Binary, VarChar, NVarChar, and VarBinary link columns for which the Length attribute is not set.

source.name: _session._default_length_for_long_columns
source.label: Default length for long columns
source.description: Enter the default length for the LongVarChar, LongNVarChar and LongVarBinary link columns for which the Length attribute is not set.

source.name: _session._fetch_size
source.label: Fetch size
source.description: Specify the number of rows that the driver must try to fetch from the data source when the connector requests a single row. Fetching rows in addition to the row requested by the connector can improve performance because the driver can complete the subsequent requests for more rows from the connector locally without a need to access the data source. The default value is 0, which indicates that the driver optimizes the fetch operation based on its internal logic.

source.name: _session._generate_all_columns_as_unicode
source.label: Generate all columns as unicode
source.description: Always generate columns as NChar, NVarChar and LongNVarChar columns instead of Char, VarChar and LongVarChar columns.

source.name: _session._keep_conductor_connection_alive
source.label: Keep conductor connection alive
source.description: Select Yes to keep the connection alive in the conductor process while the player processes are processing records. Select No to close the connection in the conductor process before player processes start processing records, and to connect again if necessary after the player processes complete processing the records.

source.name: _session._report_schema_mismatch
source.label: Report schema mismatch
source.description: Select Yes to perform early comparison of the column definitions on the link with the column definitions in the data source and to issue warning messages for any detected discrepancies which can result in data corruption. Depending on the environment and the usage scenario the early detection of discrepancies may not be possible in which case the error messages are reported only when the actual data corruption is detected.

source.name: _table_name
source.label: Table name
source.description: Enter the fully qualified name of the table that you want to access in the data source.

source.name: add_proccode_column
source.label: Add procedure return value to schema
source.description: Adds the ProcCode column which consists of the procedure return code to the schema.

source.name: before_after.after
source.label: After SQL
source.description: Enter the SQL statement to run once after all data is processed.

source.name: before_after.after.fail_on_error
source.label: Fail on error After SQL
source.description: Abort the job if there is an error executing a command

source.name: before_after.after_node
source.label: After SQL (node)
source.description: Enter the SQL statement to run once on each node after all data is processed on that node.

source.name: before_after.after_node.fail_on_error
source.label: Fail on error After SQL (node)
source.description: Abort the job if there is an error executing a command

source.name: before_after.before
source.label: Before SQL
source.description: Enter the SQL statement to run once before any data is processed.

source.name: before_after.before.fail_on_error
source.label: Fail on error Before SQL
source.description: Abort the job if there is an error executing a command

source.name: before_after.before_node
source.label: Before SQL (node)
source.description: Enter the SQL statement to run once on each node before any data is processed on that node.

source.name: before_after.before_node.fail_on_error
source.label: Fail on error Before SQL (node)
source.description: Abort the job if there is an error executing a command

source.name: byte_limit
source.label: Byte limit
source.description: The maximum number of bytes to return. Use any of following suffixes to change the unit: KB, MB, GB, or TB. A value of 0 returns all data.

source.name: call_statement
source.label: Call procedure statement
source.description: The SQL statement to execute the stored procedure

source.name: decimal_rounding_mode
source.label: Decimal Rounding Mode
source.description: Different rounding modes for values in columns of decimal and numeric data types

source.name: default_max_string_binary_precision
source.label: Default maximum length for columns
source.description: Default maximum length for columns. This property can be set/is applicable only when runtime column propagation is used

source.name: error_fatal
source.label: Fatal error
source.description: Specify error codes that should be treated as fatal.

source.name: error_warning
source.label: Warning
source.description: Specify error codes that should be treated as warning.

source.name: forward_row_data
source.label: Forward row data
source.description: Move all input columns to the Output link

source.name: generate_unicode_columns
source.label: Generate unicode type columns
source.description: Generate unicode type columns. This property can be set/is applicable only when runtime column propagation is used

source.name: has_ref_output
source.label: Has reference output
source.description: Set to true if stage has an output reference link

source.name: key_column_names
source.label: Key column names
source.description: A comma separated list of column names to override the primary key used for partitioning. The connector algorithm will try to select partitioning index from this list.

source.name: lookup_type
source.label: Lookup Type
source.description: Lookup Type

source.name: procedure_name
source.label: Procedure name
source.description: The name of the stored procedure to be executed

source.name: push_filters
source.label: Push filters
source.description: Filters as Spark SQL to be pushed to the data source

source.name: pushed_filters
source.label: Pushed filters
source.description: Inferred property that returns filters as Spark SQL that were pushed to the data source

source.name: query_timeout
source.label: Query Timeout
source.description: Specify the Query Timeout. If not specified the default value of 300 seconds or 5 minutes will be used.

source.name: rcp
source.label: Infer schema
source.description: Obtain the schema from the data source

source.name: read_mode
source.label: Read method
source.description: The method for reading records from the table

source.name: rejected_filters
source.label: Rejected filters
source.description: Inferred property that returns filters as Spark SQL that could not be pushed to the data source and still need to be evaluated

source.name: row_limit
source.label: Row limit
source.description: The maximum number of rows to return

source.name: sampling_percentage
source.label: Sampling percentage
source.description: Percentage for each row or block to be included in the sample

source.name: sampling_seed
source.label: Sampling seed
source.description: Seed to be used for getting a repeatable sample

source.name: sampling_type
source.label: Sampling type
source.description: Indicates which data sampling type should be used in the select statement

source.name: schema_name
source.label: Schema name
source.description: The name of the schema that contains the table to read from

source.name: select_statement
source.label: Select statement
source.description: The SQL SELECT statement for retrieving data from the table

source.name: static_after_partition_sql
source.label: Partition After SQL
source.description: Multiple SQL statements separated by semicolons. Each statement will be run after each partition query when the data is fetched.

source.name: static_after_partition_sql_fail_on_error
source.label: Fail on error After Partition SQL
source.description: Cancel the job if there is an error while running the After Partition SQL statement.

source.name: static_before_partition_sql
source.label: Partition Before SQL
source.description: Multiple SQL statements separated by semicolons. The statements will be run before each partition query when the data is fetched.

source.name: static_before_partition_sql_fail_on_error
source.label: Fail on error Before Partition SQL
source.description: Cancel the job if there is an error while running the Before Partition SQL statement.

source.name: static_before_sql
source.label: Before SQL
source.description: Multiple SQL statements separated by semicolons. Each statement will be run one time in the setup phase before the data is fetched.

source.name: static_before_sql_fail_on_error
source.label: Fail on error Before SQL
source.description: Cancel the job if there is an error while running the Before SQL statement.

source.name: table_name
source.label: Table name
source.description: The name of the table to read from

source.name: transform
source.label: Transform
source.description: Specifies if the transform capability is used

source.name: user_defined_function
source.label: User defined function
source.description: Specify if a user defined function is expected.

target.name: _additional_properties
target.label: Additional properties
target.description: The JDBC properties used for interaction with data source (multi-line key=value format).

target.name: _auto_commit_mode

target.description: Configure the stage to run in auto-commit mode. In auto-commit mode, the transaction is committed automatically after each statement is executed. When the connector writes records to the data source, the transaction is committed after each row is written to the data source. When the stage is configured to run multiple statements on each row, the transaction is committed after each statement is executed on the row

target.name: _before_after
target.label: Run before and after SQL statements
target.description: Select Yes to run SQL statements before and after data is accessed in the database.

target.name: _before_after._after_sql
target.label: After SQL statement
target.description: Enter the SQL statement to run once after all of the data is processed.

target.name: _before_after._after_sql._fail_on_error
target.label: Stop the job when after SQL statement fails
target.description: Select Yes to stop the job if the After SQL statement fails.

target.name: _before_after._after_sql._read_from_file_after_sql
target.label: Read After SQL statement from the file
target.description: Select Yes to read the SQL statement from the file that is specified in the After SQL statement property.

target.name: _before_after._after_sql_node
target.label: After SQL (node) statement
target.description: Enter the SQL statement to run once on each node after all of the data is processed on that node.

target.name: _before_after._after_sql_node._fail_on_error
target.label: Stop the job when after SQL (node) statement fails
target.description: Select Yes to stop the job if the After SQL (node) statement fails.

target.name: _before_after._after_sql_node._read_from_file_after_sql_node
target.label: Read After SQL (node) statement from file
target.description: Select Yes to read the SQL statement from the file that is specified in the After SQL (node) statement property.

target.name: _before_after._before_sql
target.label: Before SQL statement
target.description: Enter the SQL statement to run once before any data is processed.

target.name: _before_after._before_sql._fail_on_error
target.label: Stop the job when before SQL statement fails
target.description: Select Yes to stop the job if the Before SQL statement fails.

target.name: _before_after._before_sql._read_from_file_before_sql
target.label: Read Before SQL statement from file
target.description: Select Yes to read the SQL statement from the file that is specified in the Before SQL statement property.

target.name: _before_after._before_sql_node
target.label: Before SQL (node) statement
target.description: Enter the SQL statement to run once on each node before any data is processed on that node.

target.name: _before_after._before_sql_node._fail_on_error
target.label: Stop the job when before SQL (node) statement fails
target.description: Select Yes to stop the job if the Before SQL (node) statement fails.

target.name: _before_after._before_sql_node._read_from_file_before_sql_node
target.label: Read Before SQL (node) statement from file
target.description: Select Yes to read the SQL statement from the file that is specified in the Before SQL (node) statement property.

target.name: _begin_end_sql
target.label: Run begin and end SQL statements
target.description: Select Yes to run SQL statements every time when a transaction begins and every time before a transaction ends

target.name: _begin_sql
target.label: Begin SQL statement
target.description: Enter the SQL statement to run one time before any records are processed in the transaction

target.name: _custom_statements
target.label: Custom statements
target.description: Custom statements to be run for each input row

target.name: _custom_statements._read_from_file_custom
target.label: Read custom statements from file
target.description: Select Yes to read the custom statements from the file that is specified in the Custom statements property.

target.name: _delete_statement
target.label: Delete statement
target.description: Enter a DELETE statement. The statement is used to delete rows from the database.

target.name: _delete_statement._read_from_file_delete
target.label: Read delete statement from file
target.description: Select Yes to read the DELETE statement from the file that is specified in the DELETE statement property.

target.name: _enable_quoted_ids
target.label: Enable quoted identifiers
target.description: Select Yes to enclose the specified table name and column names on the links in quoting strings when SQL statements are generated. The connector queries the driver to determine the quoting string. If it fails to obtain this information from the driver, the connector uses the backtick (`) character as the quoting string. The default is No.

target.name: _end_sql
target.label: End SQL statement
target.description: Enter the SQL statement to run one time in the transaction after all the records were processed in the transaction and before the transaction completes successfully

target.name: _generate_sql
target.label: Generate SQL at run time
target.description: Select Yes to automatically generate the SQL statements at run time.

target.name: _insert_statement
target.label: Insert statement
target.description: Enter an INSERT statement. The statement is used to insert rows into the database.

target.name: _insert_statement._read_from_file_insert
target.label: Read insert statement from file
target.description: Select Yes to read the INSERT statement from the file that is specified in the INSERT statement property.

target.name: _isolation_level
target.label: Isolation level
target.description: Specify how the connector manages statements in transactions. As soon as the connector establishes a connection and issues the first transactional statement, the connector implicitly starts a transaction that uses the specified isolation level

target.name: _java._heap_size
target.label: Heap size
target.description: Specify the maximum Java Virtual Machine heap size in megabytes.

target.name: _load_from_file._azure._encryption
target.label: Encryption
target.description: Specify the Encryption method (either NONE or AZURE_CSE). If no value is provided then it would be considered as NONE

target.name: _load_from_file._azure._file_format_name
target.label: File format name
target.description: Specify the name of the predefined file format to be used for this load

target.name: _load_from_file._azure._file_name
target.label: File name
target.description: Specify the fully qualified filename in the container/folder/filename format

target.name: _load_from_file._azure._master_key
target.label: Master key
target.description: Specify the Master Key , required when Encryption = AZURE_CSE

target.name: _load_from_file._azure._sastoken
target.label: SAS token
target.description: Specify the SAS token required for connecting to Azure Storage

target.name: _load_from_file._azure._storage_area_name
target.label: Azure storage account name
target.description: Specify the name of the Azure Storage account name

target.name: _load_from_file._azure._use_existing_file_format
target.label: Use existing file format
target.description: Specify whether to use an existing file format or use the File format options when creating the stage

target.name: _load_from_file._copy_options._on_error
target.label: On error
target.description: Specify to continue or abort the load if an error occurs

target.name: _load_from_file._copy_options._other_copy_options
target.label: Other copy options
target.description: Specify other copy options to be used while loading from S3

target.name: _load_from_file._create_staging_area
target.label: Create staging area
target.description: Specify whether to create staging area or use an existing one

target.name: _load_from_file._credentials_file_name
target.label: Credentials file
target.description: Specify the file name along with the path that contains the access key and secret key in the format aws_access_key_id=AWS access key and aws_secret_access_key=AWS secret key. aws_access_key_id and aws_secret_access_key should be in two lines. If a value is not specified for this property, the credentials must be specified for the Access key and Secret key properties

target.name: _load_from_file._delete_staging_area
target.label: Delete staging area
target.description: Select no if the staging area has to be retained, by default it is deleted.

target.name: _load_from_file._directory_path
target.label: Directory path
target.description: Directory path to which temporary files required for PUT can be written to

target.name: _load_from_file._file_format
target.label: File format options
target.description: Specify file format options for when using external staging location

target.name: _load_from_file._file_format._binary_as_text
target.label: Binary as text
target.description: Select to enable binary as text

target.name: _load_from_file._file_format._compression
target.label: Compression
target.description: Specify the compression used for the files

target.name: _load_from_file._file_format._date_format
target.label: Date format
target.description: Specify the Date Format

target.name: _load_from_file._file_format._encoding
target.label: Encoding
target.description: Specify the Encoding

target.name: _load_from_file._file_format._field_delimiter
target.label: Field delimiter
target.description: Specify the Field Delimiter

target.name: _load_from_file._file_format._other_format_options
target.label: Other format options
target.description: Specify other format options apart from the ones listed above in option=value format

target.name: _load_from_file._file_format._record_delimiter
target.label: Record delimiter
target.description: Specify the Record Delimiter

target.name: _load_from_file._file_format._skip_byte_order_mark
target.label: Skip byte order mark(BOM)
target.description: Select to skip the Byte Order Mark(BOM)

target.name: _load_from_file._file_format._snappy_compression
target.label: Snappy compression
target.description: Select to enable Snappy Compression

target.name: _load_from_file._file_format._time_format
target.label: Time format
target.description: Specify the Time Format

target.name: _load_from_file._file_format._timestamp_format
target.label: Timestamp format
target.description: Specify the Timestamp Format

target.name: _load_from_file._gcs._file_format
target.label: File format name
target.description: Specify the name of the predefined file format to be used for this load. The specified file format should exist in the database

target.name: _load_from_file._gcs._file_name
target.label: File URL
target.description: Specify the URL path of the file (bucket/folder/filename)

target.name: _load_from_file._gcs._storage_integration
target.label: Stage integration name
target.description: Specify the name of the Google cloud storage stage integration. This integration has to be created outside in Snowflake database

target.name: _load_from_file._gcs._use_existing_file_format
target.label: Use existing file format
target.description: Specify whether to use an existing file format or use the File format options when creating the stage

target.name: _load_from_file._max_file_size
target.label: Maximum file size
target.description: The maximum file size in MB for the staging area file after which a new file would be created. The default value of 64 indicates the staging file created on the local disk would be of size 64MB. This property can be tuned to get an optimal performance.

target.name: _load_from_file._purge_copied_files
target.label: Purge files after loading
target.description: Specify whether to purge the files which are copied into the table from the staging area. For external staging area, PURGE=TRUE would delete the file from the bucket after loading the file

target.name: _load_from_file._s3._access_key
target.label: Access key
target.description: Specify the Amazon Web Services access key

target.name: _load_from_file._s3._bucket_name
target.label: Bucket name
target.description: Specify the S3 bucket name

target.name: _load_from_file._s3._encryption
target.label: Encryption
target.description: Specify Encryption

target.name: _load_from_file._s3._file_name
target.label: S3 file name
target.description: Specify the location of the S3 file name from which data needs to be moved to table

target.name: _load_from_file._s3._secret_key
target.label: Secret key
target.description: Specify the Amazon Web Services secret key

target.name: _load_from_file._staging_area_format._encoding
target.label: Encoding
target.description: Specify the Encoding

target.name: _load_from_file._staging_area_format._escape_character
target.label: Escape
target.description: Specify the character to use to escape field and row delimiters. If an escape character exists in the data, the escape character is also escaped. Because escape characters require additional processing, do not specify a value for this property if you do not need to include escape characters in the data

target.name: _load_from_file._staging_area_format._field_delimiter
target.label: Field delimiter
target.description: Specify the Field Delimiter

target.name: _load_from_file._staging_area_format._null_value
target.label: Null value
target.description: Specify the character or string that represents null values in the data. When writing to the file in staging area, null values are represented by the value that is specified for this property. This value would be assigned to NULL_IF attribute which will be used for both creating the stage and in COPY command.

target.name: _load_from_file._staging_area_format._other_file_format_options
target.label: Other file format options
target.description: Specify format options apart from the ones listed above which will be used either in creating the staging area or in the execution of COPY command. If Create staging area=Yes, then these format options will be used when creating the staging area and if  Create staging area = NO, then these format options will be used directly in the execution of COPY command. The options should be specified in the option=value format, for example EMPTY_FIELD_AS_NULL=TRUE, if more than one option is to be specified then provide  a space between the two options. For list of File format options available please refer Snowflake documentation

target.name: _load_from_file._staging_area_format._quotes
target.label: Quotes
target.description: Fields optionally enclosed by - Character used to enclose strings. Value can be NONE, single quote character ('), or double quote character (")

target.name: _load_from_file._staging_area_format._record_delimiter
target.label: Record Delimiter
target.description: Specify a string or one of the following values: <NL>, <CR>, <LF>, <TAB>. The string can include Unicode escape strings in the form \uNNNN where NNNN is the Unicode character code

target.name: _load_from_file._staging_area_name
target.label: Staging area name
target.description: Specify the external staging area name

target.name: _load_from_file._staging_area_type
target.label: Staging area type
target.description: The type of staging area either Snowflake managed or Externally managed one

target.name: _load_from_file._use_credentials_file
target.label: Use credentials file
target.description: Select whether to use a file that contains the access key and secret key when the connector connects to Amazon S3

target.name: _record_count
target.label: Record count
target.description: Specify the number of rows that the stage reads from or writes to the data source in a single transaction. When this property is set to 0, the transaction is committed only once on each processing node of the stage after the stage processes all the rows on that node. When rows arrive on the input link of the stage in waves, the Record count value applies to each wave separately

target.name: _run_end_sql_if_no_records_processed
target.label: Run End SQL statement if no records processed
target.description: Select Yes to run the End SQL statement irrespective of the number of records processed in the transaction. Select No to run the End SQL statement only if one or more records were processed in the transaction

target.name: _session._array_size
target.label: Array size
target.description: Array size

target.name: _session._batch_size
target.label: Batch size
target.description: Enter the number of records to include in the batch of records for each statement execution. The value 0 indicates that all input records are passed to the statements in a single batch.

target.name: _session._character_set_for_non_unicode_columns
target.label: Character set for non-unicode columns
target.description: Select the character set option for the values of Char, VarChar and LongVarChar link columns for which the Extended attribute is not set to Unicode. If you select the Default option, the character set encoding of the engine host system locale is used. If you select the Custom option, you must provide the character set name to be used.

target.name: _session._character_set_for_non_unicode_columns._character_set_name
target.label: Character set name
target.description: Specify the name of the character set encoding for the values of Char, VarChar and LongVarChar link columns for which the Extended attribute is not set to Unicode.

target.name: _session._default_length_for_columns
target.label: Default length for columns
target.description: Enter the default length for the Char, NChar, Binary, VarChar, NVarChar, and VarBinary link columns for which the Length attribute is not set.

target.name: _session._default_length_for_long_columns
target.label: Default length for long columns
target.description: Enter the default length for the LongVarChar, LongNVarChar and LongVarBinary link columns for which the Length attribute is not set.

target.name: _session._drop_unmatched_fields
target.label: Drop unmatched fields
target.description: Select Yes to drop any fields from the input link for which there are no matching parameters in the statements configured for the stage. Select No to issue error message when an unmatched field is present on the link.

target.name: _session._keep_conductor_connection_alive
target.label: Keep conductor connection alive
target.description: Select Yes to keep the connection alive in the conductor process while the player processes are processing records. Select No to close the connection in the conductor process before player processes start processing records, and to connect again if necessary after the player processes complete processing the records.

target.name: _session._report_schema_mismatch
target.label: Report schema mismatch
target.description: Select Yes to perform early comparison of the column definitions on the link with the column definitions in the data source and to issue warning messages for any detected discrepancies which can result in data corruption. Depending on the environment and the usage scenario the early detection of discrepancies may not be possible in which case the error messages are reported only when the actual data corruption is detected.

target.name: _table_action
target.label: Table action
target.description: Select the action to complete before writing data to the table.

target.name: _table_action._generate_create_statement
target.label: Generate create table statement at run time
target.description: Select Yes to automatically generate the CREATE TABLE statement at run time. Depending on the input link column data types, the driver, and the data source in question, the connector may not be able to determine the corresponding native data types and produce a valid statement

target.name: _table_action._generate_create_statement._create_statement
target.label: Create table statement
target.description: Enter the CREATE TABLE statement to run to create the target database table

target.name: _table_action._generate_create_statement._fail_on_error
target.label: Stop the job when create table statement fails
target.description: Select Yes to stop the job if the CREATE TABLE statement fails

target.name: _table_action._generate_drop_statement
target.label: Generate drop table statement at run time
target.description: Select Yes to automatically generate the DROP TABLE statement at run time.

target.name: _table_action._generate_drop_statement._drop_statement
target.label: Drop table statement
target.description: Enter the DROP TABLE statement to run to drop the target database table.

target.name: _table_action._generate_drop_statement._fail_on_error
target.label: Stop the job when drop table statement fails
target.description: Select Yes to stop the job if the DROP TABLE statement fails.

target.name: _table_action._generate_truncate_statement
target.label: Generate truncate table statement at run time
target.description: Select Yes to automatically generate the TRUNCATE TABLE statement at run time.

target.name: _table_action._generate_truncate_statement._fail_on_error
target.label: Stop the job when truncate table statement fails
target.description: Select Yes to stop the job if the TRUNCATE TABLE statement fails.

target.name: _table_action._generate_truncate_statement._truncate_statement
target.label: Truncate table statement
target.description: Enter the TRUNCATE TABLE statement to run to truncate the target database table.

target.name: _table_action._table_action_first
target.label: Perform table action first
target.description: Select Yes to perform the table action first. Select No to run the Before SQL statements first.

target.name: _table_name
target.label: Table name
target.description: Enter the fully qualified name of the table that you want to access in the data source.

target.name: _update_statement
target.label: Update statement
target.description: Enter an UPDATE statement. The statement is used to update rows in the database.

target.name: _update_statement._read_from_file_update
target.label: Read update statement from file
target.description: Select Yes to read the UPDATE statement from the file that is specified in the UPDATE statement property.

target.name: _use_merge_statement
target.label: Use merge statement
target.description: Specify Yes to use Snowflake merge statement functionality for  Update, Delete, Insert then Update and Delete then Insert write modes. If No is selected Snowflake driver functionality for these write modes will be invoked

target.name: _write_mode
target.label: Write mode
target.description: Select the mode that you want to use to write to the data source

target.name: azure_encryption
target.label: Encryption
target.description: Specify the Encryption method (either NONE or AZURE_CSE). If no value is provided then it would be considered as NONE

target.name: azure_file_name
target.label: File name
target.description: Specify the fully qualified filename in the container/folder/filename format

target.name: azure_master_key
target.label: Master key
target.description: Specify the Master Key , required when Encryption = AZURE_CSE

target.name: azure_sastoken
target.label: SAS token
target.description: Specify the SAS token required for connecting to Azure Storage

target.name: azure_storage_area_name
target.label: Azure storage account name
target.description: Specify the name of the Azure Storage account name

target.name: batch_size
target.label: Batch size
target.description: Number of records to be included in each batch

target.name: before_after.after
target.label: After SQL
target.description: Enter the SQL statement to run once after all data is processed.

target.name: before_after.after.fail_on_error
target.label: Fail on error After SQL
target.description: Abort the job if there is an error executing a command

target.name: before_after.after_node
target.label: After SQL (node)
target.description: Enter the SQL statement to run once on each node after all data is processed on that node.

target.name: before_after.after_node.fail_on_error
target.label: Fail on error After SQL (node)
target.description: Abort the job if there is an error executing a command

target.name: before_after.before
target.label: Before SQL
target.description: Enter the SQL statement to run once before any data is processed.

target.name: before_after.before.fail_on_error
target.label: Fail on error Before SQL
target.description: Abort the job if there is an error executing a command

target.name: before_after.before_node
target.label: Before SQL (node)
target.description: Enter the SQL statement to run once on each node before any data is processed on that node.

target.name: before_after.before_node.fail_on_error
target.label: Fail on error Before SQL (node)
target.description: Abort the job if there is an error executing a command

target.name: copy_options_on_error
target.label: On error
target.description: Specify to continue or abort the load if an error occurs

target.name: create_staging_area
target.label: Create staging area
target.description: Specify whether to create staging area or use an existing one

target.name: create_statement
target.label: Create statement
target.description: The Create DDL statement for recreating the target table

target.name: delete_staging_area
target.label: Delete staging area
target.description: Select no if the staging area has to be retained, by default it is deleted.

target.name: directory_path
target.label: Directory path
target.description: Directory path to which temporary files required for PUT can be written to

target.name: existing_table_action
target.label: Table action
target.description: The action to take on the target table, if it already exists, to handle the existing and new data sets

target.name: file_format
target.label: File format options
target.description: Specify file format options for when using external staging location

target.name: file_format_binary_as_text
target.label: Binary as text
target.description: Select to enable binary as text

target.name: file_format_compression
target.label: Compression
target.description: Specify the compression used for the files

target.name: file_format_date_format
target.label: Date format
target.description: Specify the Date Format

target.name: file_format_encoding
target.label: Encoding
target.description: Specify the Encoding

target.name: file_format_field_delimiter
target.label: Field delimiter
target.description: Specify the Field Delimiter

target.name: file_format_name
target.label: File format name
target.description: Specify the name of the predefined file format to be used for this load. The specified file format should exist in the database

target.name: file_format_other_format_options
target.label: Other format options
target.description: Specify other format options apart from the ones listed above in option=value format

target.name: file_format_record_delimiter
target.label: Record delimiter
target.description: Specify the Record Delimiter

target.name: file_format_skip_byte_order_mark
target.label: Skip byte order mark(BOM)
target.description: Select to skip the Byte Order Mark(BOM)

target.name: file_format_snappy_compression
target.label: Snappy compression
target.description: Select to enable Snappy Compression

target.name: file_format_time_format
target.label: Time format
target.description: Specify the Time Format

target.name: file_format_timestamp_format
target.label: Timestamp format
target.description: Specify the Timestamp Format

target.name: gcs_file_name
target.label: File URL
target.description: Specify the URL path of the file (bucket/folder/filename)

target.name: has_reject_output
target.label: Has reject output
target.description: Set to true if stage has an output reject link

target.name: key_column_names
target.label: Key column names
target.description: A comma separated list of column names to override the primary key used during an update or merge

target.name: max_file_size
target.label: Maximum file size
target.description: The maximum file size in MB for the staging area file after which a new file would be created. The default value of 64 indicates the staging file created on the local disk would be of size 64MB. This property can be tuned to get an optimal performance.

target.name: other_copy_options
target.label: Other copy options
target.description: Specify other copy options to be used

target.name: purge_copied_files
target.label: Purge files after loading
target.description: Specify whether to purge the files which are copied into the table from the staging area. For external staging area, PURGE=TRUE would delete the file from the bucket after loading the file

target.name: query_timeout
target.label: Query Timeout
target.description: Specify the Query Timeout. If not specified the default value of 300 seconds or 5 minutes will be used.

target.name: s3_access_key
target.label: Access key
target.description: Specify the Amazon Web Services access key

target.name: s3_bucket_name
target.label: Bucket name
target.description: Specify the S3 bucket name

target.name: s3_encryption
target.label: Encryption
target.description: Specify Encryption

target.name: s3_file_name
target.label: S3 file name
target.description: Specify the location of the S3 file name from which data needs to be moved to table

target.name: s3_secret_key
target.label: Secret key
target.description: Specify the Amazon Web Services secret key

target.name: schema_name
target.label: Schema name
target.description: The name of the schema that contains the table to write to

target.name: staging_area_file_encoding
target.label: Encoding
target.description: Specify the Encoding

target.name: staging_area_file_escape_character
target.label: Escape
target.description: Specify the character to use to escape field and row delimiters. If an escape character exists in the data, the escape character is also escaped. Because escape characters require additional processing, do not specify a value for this property if you do not need to include escape characters in the data

target.name: staging_area_file_field_delimiter
target.label: Field delimiter
target.description: Specify the Field Delimiter

target.name: staging_area_file_format
target.label: File Format
target.description: Choose Format types

target.name: staging_area_file_null_value
target.label: Null value
target.description: Specify the character or string that represents null values in the data. When writing to the file in staging area, null values are represented by the value that is specified for this property. This value would be assigned to NULL_IF attribute which will be used for both creating the stage and in COPY command.

target.name: staging_area_file_quotes
target.label: Quotes
target.description: Fields optionally enclosed by - Character used to enclose strings. Value can be NONE, single quote character ('), or double quote character (")

target.name: staging_area_file_record_delimiter
target.label: Record Delimiter
target.description: Specify a string or one of the following values: <NL>, <CR>, <LF>, <TAB>. The string can include Unicode escape strings in the form \uNNNN where NNNN is the Unicode character code

target.name: staging_area_name
target.label: Staging area name
target.description: Specify the external staging area name

target.name: staging_area_other_file_format_options
target.label: Other file format options
target.description: Specify format options apart from the ones listed above which will be used either in creating the staging area or in the execution of COPY command. If Create staging area=Yes, then these format options will be used when creating the staging area and if  Create staging area = NO, then these format options will be used directly in the execution of COPY command. The options should be specified in the option=value format, for example EMPTY_FIELD_AS_NULL=TRUE, if more than one option is to be specified then provide  a space between the two options. For list of File format options available please refer Snowflake documentation

target.name: staging_area_type
target.label: Staging area type
target.description: The type of staging area, either internal staging area where the storage is managed internally by Snowflake or external staging area where the storage points to data in storage services like Amazon S3, Azure Blob, or Google Cloud Storage.

target.name: static_statement
target.label: Static statement
target.description: The SQL used for setup operations, for example a CREATE statement

target.name: storage_integration_name
target.label: Storage integration name
target.description: Storage integration name

target.name: table_action
target.label: Table action
target.description: The action to take on the target table to handle the new data set

target.name: table_name
target.label: Table name
target.description: The name of the table to write to

target.name: update_statement
target.label: Update statement
target.description: The SQL INSERT, UPDATE, MERGE, or DELETE statement for updating data in the table.

target.name: use_existing_file_format
target.label: Use existing file format
target.description: Specify whether to use an existing file format or use the File format options when creating the stage

target.name: use_storage_integration
target.label: Use storage integration
target.description: Use storage integration

target.name: write_mode
target.label: Write mode
target.description: The mode for writing records to the target table
